cards.text: Malaysian palm oil to China fell 39% before Trump met Xi, and the cost lands on 210,000 smallholders
cards.sub: Plantation Minister Johari attributed the drop to price (Malaysian CPO at one point USD120 a tonne above soybean oil), not the summit. Mill-gate prices track Bursa CPO directly to smallholder income.

cards.h: Palm oil traded USD120 a tonne above soybean oil at one point in 2025, and Chinese refiners shifted mix
cards.s: Malaysia's 2024 exports to China were 1.39 million tonnes, down 5.3% on 2023. The Jan-Oct 2025 drop hit 39%. Reuters reported the full-year 2025 China decline at 35.7%.
cards.d: Plantation and Commodities Minister Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani told the Malaysian Palm Oil Council on 27 November 2025 that the cause of the 39% drop in Malaysia's China exports over the first ten months of 2025 was price. Malaysian crude palm oil at one point traded at a USD120 per tonne premium to soybean oil during the year. Reuters later reported the full-year 2025 decline to China at 35.7%. China's edible-oil mix is structurally weighted toward soybean and rapeseed oil with palm oil in third place; when the palm-soy spread inverts, Chinese refiners shift mix. Reuters and industry reporting also cite cheaper Indonesian palm oil and logistics as contributing factors alongside the price spread Johari named.

cards.h: China signed an oilseed deal with Brazil worth more than US$10 billion in November 2025
cards.s: COFCO inked agreements reported at roughly 20 million tonnes of soybeans, soybean oil and palm oil with ADM, Bunge, Cargill and Louis Dreyfus at the Shanghai Import Expo. Signed contracts, not delivered cargo.
cards.d: China's state oilseed trader COFCO signed multi-billion-dollar import agreements at the China International Import Expo in Shanghai in November 2025, reported by industry trackers at approximately 20 million tonnes of Brazilian soybeans, soybean oil and palm oil with a total contract value above US$10 billion. The counterparties were ADM, Bunge, Cargill and Louis Dreyfus. COFCO's own news page confirms expo signing activity with major traders; the exact tonnage and dollar value are reported, not directly verified from the original detailed contract text. Signed import agreements differ from delivered supply: capacity is reserved on paper, not yet on water. Separately, US soybean exports to China fell sharply during the 2024-25 trade-friction window, leaving Brazil to absorb the displaced demand.

cards.h: Greer announced the deal. China's foreign ministry said working teams were still in consultation
cards.s: Greer told Bloomberg China would buy 'double-digit billions per year for three years.' Wang Yi said specifics were under consultation. July soybean futures fell 36 cents a bushel that day.
cards.d: On 15 May 2026 US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer told Bloomberg that China was expected to commit to double-digit billion dollar agricultural purchases over the next three years, per year, covering soybeans, corn, sorghum, milling wheat, beef and poultry. China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs readout the same day said working teams from both countries were still in consultation on the relevant details and would lock in outcomes expeditiously. Market reaction was negative on the missing product-level specifics: July soybean futures dropped more than 36 cents per bushel. Boeing landed an order for 200 aircraft, of a number Trump had floated as high as 750 ahead of and during the summit. The October 2025 prior arrangement, which committed China to purchase at least 12 million tonnes of US soybeans before year-end and at least 25 million tonnes annually in 2026-2028, sets the floor against which the May 2026 announcement gets read.

cards.h: When CPO falls, the corporate listcos hedge. Smallholders absorb it
cards.s: About 210,000 smallholders cultivate ~15% of the planted area. Kenya overtook the EU as #2 buyer in H1 2025. The Oct 2025 US-Malaysia trade deal exempted palm oil. April oleochemicals rose 23.83%.
cards.d: Roughly 210,000 independent oil palm smallholders cultivate about 15% of Malaysia's planted oil palm area. The PNB Research Institute's September 2025 flagship report documents the political-economy reality of this sector: smallholders are paid mill-gate prices that track Bursa CPO directly, face ageing trees, high input costs, and the burden of sustaining MSPO certification. Corporate integrated planters and millers can hedge, diversify revenue, or access financing during prolonged demand weakness; smallholders cannot. The government's response to the China drop is not absent. In the first half of 2025 Kenya overtook the European Union as Malaysia's second-largest palm oil buyer per MPOC. The October 2025 US-Malaysia Reciprocal Trade Agreement, signed at the ASEAN Kuala Lumpur summit, exempted 1,711 Malaysian tariff lines, including palm oil, from the US 19% tariff regime. The April 2026 MPOB release showed oleochemical exports up 23.83% and biodiesel up 193.04% month-on-month, even as raw palm oil exports fell 14.34%. Structural pressure is older: Malaysia's replanting rate sits at 1.8% against the government's 4% target, dependency on foreign workers in the upstream remains around 75%, and downstream-pivot revenue takes years to scale where mill-gate prices move in weeks.

cards.text: The summit did not break Malaysian palm oil. Price did, in 2025, as the minister attributed. The new question is whether Malaysia's rerouting outpaces the cost falling on 210,000 smallholders.

Mainstream framing puts the Trump-Xi summit at the centre of any new threat to Malaysian palm oil. The evidence puts it at the margin. China demand fell before the summit, primarily on price as the plantation minister attributed and as industry trackers corroborated. Brazil's COFCO locked in competing oilseed supply in November 2025. Indonesia is absorbing 15.65 million kilolitres into B40 biodiesel and accelerating to B50 on 1 July 2026. Malaysia has not been passive: Kenya overtook the EU in H1 2025, the October 2025 US-Malaysia trade agreement exempted palm oil from the 19% tariff regime, and April oleochemicals rose 23.83% even as raw CPO fell 14.34%. The structural pressure underneath the China headline is older than any summit: a replanting rate at 1.8% against the 4% target, ~75% foreign-worker dependency, and a smallholder cost that successive administrations across BN, PH and the current government have not insulated. The summit confirms a story already running. The harder story is the gap between Malaysia's trade-policy timetable and its smallholder-income timetable.

headline: Malaysian Palm Oil to China Dropped 39% Before Trump Met Xi
context: Malaysia's palm oil exports to China fell 39% in Jan-Oct 2025, six months before Trump met Xi. The plantation minister attributed the drop to price, not geopolitics. Brazil's COFCO had locked in US$10 billion of competing oilseed supply in November 2025.
sources: MPOC (Minister Johari Abdul Ghani statement, 27 November 2025); Bloomberg (USTR Greer interview, 15 May 2026); DTN Progressive Farmer (15 May 2026); Reuters via Yahoo Finance (15 May 2026); Reuters (China palm oil demand drop, 9 February 2026); Ministry of Foreign Affairs of China (Wang Yi briefing, 15 May 2026); White House Fact Sheet (US-China prior arrangement, 1 November 2025); American Soybean Association policy paper (August 2025); COFCO official news page (6 November 2025); Datamarnews; MarketScreener; Farm Policy News, University of Illinois (November 2025); Farmdoc Daily (November 2025); USDA FAS China Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026; USDA FAS Malaysia Oilseeds and Products Update (December 2025); USDA FAS Indonesia Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026; MPOB monthly statistical release April 2026 via The Edge Malaysia and The Star (12 May 2026); MPOB Overview of the Malaysian Oil Palm Industry 2024; Bursa Malaysia Derivatives (FCPO July 2026 contract); MPOC Daily Palm Oil Prices (14 May 2026); MPOB BEPI Daily CPO Price (14 May 2026); ANTARA News (Indonesia B50 mandate, 19 April 2026); RRI.co.id (19 April 2026); Kompas.id (Indonesia CPO export levy, 24 May 2025); Council of the EU press release (EUDR delay, 18 December 2025); Regulation (EU) 2025/2650 (23 December 2025); Bernama/MPOC (Kenya overtaking EU and China as MY palm oil buyer, July 2025); MPOB prestasisawit (US-MY Reciprocal Trade Agreement, 6 May 2026); The Star (US-MY RTA, 26 October 2025); PNB Research Institute flagship smallholder report (September 2025); Reuters (Trump 750 Boeing planes claim, 15 May 2026); AP (Boeing order, 15 May 2026); Al Jazeera (Day 1 Beijing summit coverage); CNN Politics live blog.
confidence: High. Every numeric claim in the published cards traces to a verifying stage. 39% China drop and USD120 premium: Stage 2 and Stage 3 verified against Johari/MPOC 27 November 2025. Reuters 35.7% full-year figure: surfaced from Stage 3 omitted_facts. COFCO Brazil deal volume and value: soften-rewritten per Stage 3 misleading-framing flag (reported, not directly contract-verified). Boeing 200/750: corrected per Stage 3 against Reuters and AP. Indonesia 15.65 million kilolitres B40: corrected per Stage 3 from the original 15.6 billion litres unit error. B50 effective date 1 July 2026: per Stage 4 ANTARA News and RRI.co.id. October 2025 US-China prior commitment (12 MMT / 25 MMT annually 2026-2028): added from Stage 3 omitted_fact citing the White House Fact Sheet of 1 November 2025. Kenya overtaking the EU as second-largest buyer in H1 2025: added from Stage 4 (Bernama/MPOC). US-Malaysia Reciprocal Trade Agreement tariff exemption: added from Stage 4 (MPOB prestasisawit; The Star). April 2026 oleochemicals +23.83% and total exports -14.34%: verified from MPOB monthly release via The Edge Malaysia. 210,000 smallholder figure and 15% planted-area share: added from Stage 4 PNB Research Institute report. Replanting rate 1.8% versus 4% target: added from Stage 4 USDA FAS Malaysia Oilseeds Update December 2025. The original Stage 1 zero new-crop export orders claim dropped entirely (Stage 3 INCORRECT, contradicted by USDA FAS Export Sales notices). The original Stage 1 China has not formally committed framing softened to the verifiable Wang Yi readout language.
revisions_made: STRUCTURAL: Dropped the analogy card (Stage 5 flagged it as weakest of the seven). Added a fourth fact card (Social lens) to surface the smallholder cost transmission and Malaysia's diversification response, which Stages 2, 4 and 5 all flagged as the central missing dimension.

STRUCTURAL: Rewrote the hook to surface the smallholder dimension alongside the 39% figure, addressing Stage 5's audience-check critique (a FELDA smallholder reading the original would feel spoken past).
STRUCTURAL: Rewrote the reframe to bridge the data finding to the smallholder consequence, addressing Stage 5's structural-cowardice flag on the reframe.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Removed entirely the Stage 1 claim 'zero new-crop export orders booked for the 2025/26 marketing year as of the summit date' (Stage 3 status: INCORRECT, m_false 0.9). USDA FAS Export Sales notices and the China Oilseeds and Products Annual 2026 confirm China had purchased or shipped 10.8 MMT toward the 12 MMT commitment as of 26 February 2026.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Softened 'China has not formally committed' (Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING) to 'China's foreign ministry said working teams were still in consultation' which matches the Chinese MFA readout language directly.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Dropped 'Malaysian CPO at RM4,200 a tonne' from the hook (Stage 3 UNVERIFIED_DETAIL — figure came from a separate MPOC price forecast, not from Johari's 27 November statement). Kept the USD120 premium, which IS attributed to Johari.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Removed the specific 'soybean oil 44%, rapeseed oil 24%, palm oil 18%' percentages (Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING — traces to AOCS article citing 2016 research). Replaced with qualitative 'structurally weighted toward soybean and rapeseed oil with palm oil in third place'.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Boeing figure '500' replaced with Trump's actual floated number of '750' (Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING — sourced to Reuters 15 May 2026). Dropped the '4% Boeing share fall' specifically (timing-sensitive and source-varied per Stage 3); kept only the verified 200-aircraft order.

CORRECTED (Stage 3): Indonesia '15.6 billion litres of palm oil into the B40 biodiesel mandate' restated as '15.65 million kilolitres into B40 biodiesel mandate' (Stage 3 MISLEADING_FRAMING — the 15.65 figure is the kilolitre biodiesel allocation, not a one-for-one volume of palm oil).
CORRECTED (Stage 3): Indonesia B50 timing 'planned for late 2026' updated to 'on 1 July 2026' per Stage 4 ANTARA News and RRI.co.id sourcing.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'US soybean exports cut by roughly 75%' (Stage 3 UNVERIFIED_DETAIL) softened to 'US soybean exports to China fell sharply during the 2024-25 trade-friction window'.
CORRECTED (Stage 3): 'Price spread, not summit politics' (Stage 3 OVERCLAIM) rephrased to 'primarily on price as the plantation minister attributed and as industry trackers corroborated'.
REPHRASED (Stage 2 economic-framing flag): Smallholder cost transmission now anchored in its own fact card rather than buried at the end of the view.

ADDED (Stage 4): Fact 4 (Social lens) builds the diversification counter-narrative — Kenya overtaking the EU as second-largest buyer in H1 2025, US-Malaysia RTA exempting palm oil, oleochemicals up 23.83% and biodiesel up 193.04% in April 2026.
ADDED (Stage 4): Replanting rate 1.8% against the 4% target (USDA FAS Malaysia Oilseeds Update December 2025) integrated into the view as the structural-pressure point.

ADDED (Stage 4): October 2025 prior US-China arrangement (12 MMT US soybeans by year-end + 25 MMT/year for 2026-2028) added from Stage 3 omitted-fact citing the White House Fact Sheet, 1 November 2025, anchoring the May 2026 announcement against the existing baseline.
ADDED (Stage 3): Reuters' full-year 2025 China decline figure of 35.7% added alongside the Jan-Oct 39% to be precise about the period framing.

STRENGTHENED (Stage 5): Fact 4 surfaces what Stage 5 called the political-economy point that successive governments did not insulate: 'mill-gate prices that track Bursa CPO directly', 'corporate integrated planters can hedge, smallholders cannot', and the structural ~75% foreign-worker dependency and replanting gap.

STRENGTHENED (Stage 5): View card names the structural pressure underneath the China headline, including the cross-administration framing (BN, PH, current government) Stage 5 wanted. Per the CLAUDE.md 3R rule, the ethnic-political-economy framing Stage 5 proposed ('Malay FELDA settlers', 'Bumiputera rural economic participation') is dropped in favour of the structural smallholder-cost point without race-keying — same call the 1mdb-recovery synthesis made on Stage 5.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): 'Mill-gate prices track Bursa CPO directly' — synthesis arithmetic linking the PNB report's documented smallholder transmission mechanism to the cited Bursa CPO daily price.

INTRODUCED (Stage 6 self-verified): The reframe was tightened to specify 'six months before' rather than 'two years entrenched' — Stage 3 noted the original 'two years entrenched' overstates the 2024 baseline (which was only down 5.3%); the sharper 39% collapse is a 2025 phenomenon.

introduced_claims.claim: Reuters reported the full-year 2025 China decline at 35.7%
introduced_claims.primary_source: Reuters, 9 February 2026 — surfaced via Stage 3 omitted_facts list; included to give the most current full-year framing alongside Johari's Jan-Oct 39%.

introduced_claims.claim: Mill-gate prices track Bursa CPO directly to smallholder income (in hook sub and Fact 4)
introduced_claims.primary_source: PNB Research Institute flagship report on oil palm smallholders, September 2025 (Stage 4 verified); BusinessToday coverage 12 September 2025.

introduced_claims.claim: Cross-administration policy continuity on smallholder protection (BN, PH, current government)
introduced_claims.primary_source: Synthesis observation grounded in Stage 5 verified attack on bipartisan policy failure; named factually without partisan attribution. Stage 5 marked this as VERIFIED against Reuters 9 Feb 2026 framing.

synthesis_notes: Four substantive integration decisions: (1) Dropped the analogy card and added a fourth fact card. Stage 5 explicitly flagged the analogy as weakest of the seven cards because it reduced a multi-year structural substitution to a metaphor that underplayed Malaysian agency. Stages 2, 4 and 5 all identified the smallholder cost transmission as the central missing dimension. Adding a fact card with the Social lens addresses all three flags directly. The 4-fact format has precedent across 4 of 1,031 published issues (1286, 1976, 1981, 1983). (2) Rewrote the hook to surface the smallholder dimension. Stage 5's audience check found that the original hook would have a FELDA smallholder feel spoken past. The new hook keeps the 39% number first (per the concreteness rule) and adds the cost-on-smallholders clause as the second clause of the same sentence. (3) Per the CLAUDE.md 3R rule (critique policy, not communities), the racial framing Stage 5 proposed ('Malay FELDA settlers', 'Bumiputera rural economic participation', 'Malay-majority sector') is dropped in favour of the structural smallholder-cost point without race-keying. The cross-administration policy point Stage 5 raised IS preserved factually in the view card (BN, PH, current government) because it implicates the political class as a class without race-keying. This is the same call the 1mdb-recovery synthesis made on Stage 5's 'Malay political elite' framing. (4) Stage 3 flagged the 'mainstream framing' meta-claim as UNVERIFIED because there was no formal media-content analysis. The view card retains the framing but anchors it to the specific verified data (Bursa CPO action on summit day, April 2026 export figures, Greer-vs-Wang-Yi gap). The claim that mainstream framing overweights the summit remains a synthesis judgement, not a primary-source claim. Stage 5 made the same observation independently (false-balance flag) and concluded the data supports the framing, so the call holds. Gender, East Malaysian, indigenous, and forced-labour perspectives raised by Stage 4 are flagged in the brief and would warrant follow-up issues; integrating all of them would have padded this one beyond the bite-size budget.
